title: pentas lanceolata subsp. quartiniana
intro: Pentas lanceolata subsp. quartiniana, also known as the Egyptian starcluster, is a flowering plant species in the Pentas genus. It is a perennial herb native to tropical regions, known for its star-shaped flowers and medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to promote overall health and well-being.
